title: leretia racemosa
intro: Leretia racemosa, also known as the Mexican tea tree or yerba de la reina, is a flowering plant native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. Its leaves and stems have been used in traditional medicine for their antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which can help to treat various health issues such as fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Leretia racemosa include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various ailments.
